titleStmt.title: Memorandum by the Attorney General (Brownell) to the President

div.note: Eisenhower Library, Eisenhower papers, Whitman file

head.hi: Memorandum by the Attorney General (Brownell) to the President

div.p: This refers to your recent memorandum on the Bricker Amendment.The President’s memorandum of May 26 to Secretary Dulles and Attorney General Brownell, p. 1852. At a conference with the Vice President and the Secretary of State today, the Vice President stated that he would talk to Senator Ferguson about the proposal to reconsider the Bricker Amendment. All three of us believe it would be unfortunate to have the Administration appear to re-open the controversy, and believe that if the matter is re-opened it might well lead to a long filibuster-type debate in the Senate, which would jeopardize the passage of many important Administration measures. This, of course, may be “counsel of perfection” and I realize that there are many factors involved.

In the meantime I am studying the legal aspects of the draft of the suggested amendment which you attached to your memorandum, with a view to giving you an answer as soon as possible.No record of a further reply by the Attorney General on this matter has been found in Department of State files or at the Eisenhower Library.
